findings.source: CI failure: golangci-lint v2.0.2 local vs v2.10.1 CI — noctx for exec.Command
findings.description: Local linter missed noctx issue because it was an older version. CI caught it after push. Version skew creates false confidence in local hooks.
findings.proposedRule: Linter versions must match between local tooling and CI. Pin versions in CI config and document install commands.

findings.source: manifest TestParse_DoesNotPanic: strings.Repeat("a: b\n", 10000) → 17.4GB peak alloc
findings.description: A 50KB YAML input caused 17.4GB peak memory allocation through the YAML parser's node tree construction (348x amplification). Reduced to 100 reps: same code path coverage, 0.01s execution.
findings.proposedRule: Size adversarial test inputs for the code path, not the parser. Profile memory before committing large-input tests.

findings.source: 4 CI failures (OOM, lint args, noctx, govulncheck) all caught too late
findings.description: Pre-push hooks mirroring CI would have caught all 4 failures locally. Added pre-push hook running build + test + lint + govulncheck.
findings.proposedRule: Pre-push hooks must run the same checks as CI. Update hooks when CI steps change.

findings.source: exec.Command vs exec.CommandContext — process group kill semantics
findings.description: exec.CommandContext only kills parent PID, not the process group. Used exec.Command with manual goroutine + Kill(-pgid, SIGKILL). Required //nolint:noctx with rationale comment.
findings.proposedRule: nolint directives require a trailing comment explaining the suppression rationale.

findings.source: 3 security WARNs (unsanitized entrypoint, unvalidated WorkDir, unbounded buffers)
findings.description: Same class of defense-in-depth issues as auth unit. Constitution rule 26 applied directly without modification.
